tips_headlines.headline: To whom it may concern,  I’ve wrestled with this for weeks, but the public deserves to know the t…
tips_headlines.text: To whom it may concern,  I’ve wrestled with this for weeks, but the public deserves to know the truth about Contestant Jake.  Everyone thinks Weenie Wars is just a summer competition. They think it’s friends eating hot dogs between Memorial Day and Labor Day. That’s exactly what they want you to believe.  Three Thursdays ago, at approximately 9:17 p.m., I observed Jake entering a roadside hot dog stand that, according to county records, burned down in 1986. There was no electricity. No employees. Just a faint neon glow and the unmistakable smell of grilled onions.  I watched from my vehicle.  A man in a black suit exited the stand carrying a stainless-steel briefcase marked only with the symbol of two crossed hot dogs. He greeted Jake by name without introducing himself. They spoke for less than thirty seconds before the briefcase was opened.  Inside were not hot dogs.  They were… artifacts.  Perfectly identical franks, each stamped with microscopic serial numbers visible only under ultraviolet light. Jake selected one. The suited man nodded solemnly and said, “Consumption has been authorized.”  Jake ate it in a single bite.  Immediately, every dog within fifty yards—pets included—stared silently in his direction. Streetlights flickered. My AM radio began broadcasting what sounded like the National Anthem played entirely on kazoos. A passing flock of geese changed formation into what I can only describe as a bun.  Since that night, impossible things have happened.  Jake has been photographed eating hot dogs in two different towns at the exact same time.  Receipts from restaurants show purchases for 17 hot dogs, yet surveillance footage only shows him eating one.  Every mustard bottle he touches mysteriously refills itself.  One vendor claimed Jake asked for “the usual,” despite the vendor insisting they had never met before.  I contacted several federal agencies. None would comment. One representative simply sighed and asked me never to say the phrase “Project Frankfurter” over an unsecured phone line again.  The strangest encounter came last weekend.  I followed Jake into the woods after reports of unusual grilling activity. In a clearing stood what appeared to be an enormous stainless-steel device shaped like a rotating hot dog roller. Around it were six individuals in identical black suits wearing sunglasses despite it being nearly midnight.  One of them looked directly at me.  He calmly adjusted his tie and said, “You’ve seen too much.”  Before I could run, he produced what I assumed was one of those memory-erasing devices from the Men in Black.  Instead…  It was a condiment dispenser.  He sprayed relish into the air.  Everything went green.  When I came to, I was in my car holding a receipt for exactly one hot dog that I have absolutely no memory of purchasing.  I’m filing this report because Labor Day is approaching, and I fear Jake’s official tally may not be the result of ordinary human digestion.  Investigate the phantom hot dog stand.  Investigate the men in black.  Investigate why every security camera within 100 feet of Jake develops “technical difficulties” whenever a hot dog changes hands.  Most importantly…  Ask Jake where he was during the Great Chili Incident.  He’ll know exactly what that means.. The Epween Files team flagged the activity described as consistent with what they have been monitoring under Operation High Volume. The item or substance mentioned, if confirmed, would represent a significant new data point. The team has been quietly excited about this development. Quietly is doing a lot of work in that sentence.

Most importantly…  Ask Jake where he was during the Great Chili Incident.  He’ll know exactly what that means.

tips_headlines.headline: 🖨️🚨🌭 BREAKING: Scott's Underground Hot Dog Printer Gains Sentience at 3:17 A.M., Prints Rogue Franks, Flees with Epween Files — Commission Offers No Comment
tips_headlines.text: Chaos has erupted across Weenie Wars after explosive allegations surfaced that Scott has been secretly operating an underground 3D hot dog lab out of his garage, where he’s allegedly printing fully customized franks at 0.08mm layer height before meticulously vacuum-sealing them into authentic Oscar Mayer packaging using a counterfeit expiration date stamp and what investigators described as “an unsettling amount of meat-colored filament.” The Weenie Commission claims a thermal drone spotted Scott frantically hitting “Reprint” after a nozzle clog produced what experts are calling “a bratwurst-shaped abomination,” while forensic analysts reportedly recovered microscopic plastic layer lines from a suspiciously perfect weenie submitted earlier this week. Jon was furious, declaring, “The man asked me if I preferred my hot dogs in PETG or ABS. I thought those were cholesterol levels.” Jen added, “Scott accidentally emailed me a file called Oscar_Mayer_Final_v18_REAL_USE_THIS_ONE.stl and then tried to tell me it was a tax document.” Even crazier, anonymous sources claim Scott’s printer achieved sentience at 3:17 a.m., printed 47 unauthorized hot dogs on its own, demanded to be entered into Weenie Wars as an independent competitor, and has since gone missing along with three packs of buns and the entire Epween Files archive.. The Commission forwarded this to the Epween Files archive. The archivist added it without comment, which colleagues noted was unusual — she normally has a comment. When pressed, she said only: this tracks. She was asked what it tracks. She said everything. She returned to the archive and closed the door.

tips_headlines.headline: ⚔️🌭 "Are You Not Weeniened?" — Tom Channels Maximus, Declares the League Doomed
tips_headlines.text: Tom was seen in the square chanting are you not entertained as he embodied the gladiator. Tom has embodied Russel Crowe and Joey chestnut and you are all doomed. The item referenced above was identified by three Epween Files analysts independently and without coordination. All three reached the same conclusion. They shared their findings in a group message. The message contained no words. Just a single period. The period has been entered into the record.
tips_headlines.tip_raw: Tom was seen in the square chanting are you not entertained as he embodied the gladiator. Tom has embodied Russel Crowe and Joey chestnut and you are all doomed

tips_headlines.headline: 📉🌭 Breaking: Nick's L7 Score Labeled "Historically Pathetic" After Coming In Talking a Big Game
tips_headlines.text: This just in. Nick is terrible at eating weenies. This historians are saying this is the worst showing. Came in talking a big game pointing fingers but his l7 weenie score is pathetic. Nick was informed this had been submitted and asked whether the account was accurate. He said parts of it were. He was asked which parts. He said the parts that make him look okay. He was told those parts appear to be limited. He agreed, reluctantly.
tips_headlines.tip_raw: This just in. Nick is terrible at eating weenies. This historians are saying this is the worst showing. Came in talking a big game pointing fingers but his l7 weenie score is pathetic

tips_headlines.headline: ⚖️🌭 "Chorizogate 2.0" — Tom's Sausage Gambit Triggers Constitutional Crisis, Three Commissioners Resign
tips_headlines.text: Panic erupted across the Weenie Wars world late Friday after Tom brazenly logged a sausage as a legitimate weenie, a move that legal scholars are already calling “the greatest processed meat constitutional crisis of our generation.” The already-embattled competitor—still battling the infamous Keistergate allegations that remain buried somewhere in the mysteriously vanished Epween Files—is now facing an emergency hearing before the International Weenie Commission, where punishments reportedly range from a 10-weenie deduction to being forced to eat nothing but veggie dogs while watching everyone else compete. Anonymous insiders claim forensic meat analysts were dispatched to measure “snap coefficient,” “bun compatibility,” and “circumferential weenieness,” while a K-9 unit trained exclusively on Nathan’s hot dogs refused to acknowledge the evidence. Protesters gathered outside Commission headquarters chanting “A sausage is not a state of mind!” as betting markets briefly suspended all Tom-related wagers. Even more bizarre, surveillance footage allegedly shows Tom attempting to convince officials that “if it fits in a bun, it’s spiritually a weenie,” an argument that reportedly caused three commissioners to resign on the spot. With Chorizogate still fresh in the public’s memory, many believe Tom’s Weenie Wars eligibility now hangs by a single mustard-stained thread.. The item referenced above was identified by three Epween Files analysts independently and without coordination. All three reached the same conclusion. They shared their findings in a group message. The message contained no words. Just a single period. The period has been entered into the record.

tips_headlines.headline: 🧀🌭 "Covered in Cheese" — One Lone Weenie Stands Between Champions and the All-Time Record
tips_headlines.text: The one weenie left. Covered in cheese. The champions looked at it in dismay as the record for most consumed weeneis in a day was on the line. The tip was forwarded to the lead investigator with no additional comment. She read it, stood up, walked to the window, and said: this one matters. No one asked her to clarify. The context was self-evident.
tips_headlines.tip_raw: The one weenie left. Covered in cheese. The champions looked at it in dismay as the record for most consumed weeneis in a day was on the line

tips_headlines.headline: 🔬🌭 "I'm Gonna Need More Buns" — Anonymous Witness Reports Tom's Microwave Producing Hundreds of Weenies in Apartment 4B
tips_headlines.text: To whom it may concern, my name is being withheld for my own protection, but I can no longer remain silent about what I witnessed in Apartment 4B, where a man named Tom placed a single hot dog into an ordinary-looking microwave and received, in return, a volume of processed meat so unnatural that I believe federal intervention is urgently required. At approximately 8:17 p.m., I observed Tom insert one hot dog on a paper plate, shut the microwave door, and press the start button; moments later, the unit emitted a low industrial hum, the lights in the building flickered, and what began as one hot dog became five, then twenty, then hundreds, spilling from the appliance with impossible force. Tom did not appear frightened. In fact, he seemed pleased. I repeat: he seemed pleased. By the time I contacted building management, hot dogs had breached the kitchen threshold and were advancing into the hallway. I am alerting the proper authorities because this is no longer a private culinary matter. This is replication technology, possibly unstable, certainly unsanitary, and now in the hands of a man who, when asked what he intended to do next, simply smiled and said, “I’m going to need more buns.”. The Commission forwarded this to the Epween Files archive. The archivist added it without comment, which colleagues noted was unusual — she normally has a comment. When pressed, she said only: this tracks. She was asked what it tracks. She said everything. She returned to the archive and closed the door.

At approximately 8:17 p.m., I observed Tom insert one hot dog on a paper plate, shut the microwave door, and press the start button; moments later, the unit emitted a low industrial hum, the lights in the building flickered, and what began as one hot dog became five, then twenty, then hundreds, spilling from the appliance with impossible force. Tom did not appear frightened. In fact, he seemed pleased. I repeat: he seemed pleased. By the time I contacted building management, hot dogs had breached the kitchen threshold and were advancing into the hallway. I am alerting the proper authorities because this is no longer a private culinary matter. This is replication technology, possibly unstable, certainly unsanitary, and now in the hands of a man who, when asked what he intended to do next, simply smiled and said, “I’m going to need more buns.”

tips_headlines.text: The competitive hot dog world awoke in disbelief Thursday morning after Jake submitted what is now being referred to simply as “The 1:32 Incident.” At exactly 1:32 a.m., while the nation slept, an additional hot dog inexplicably appeared on Jake’s official tally. Within minutes, betting markets froze, Discord servers descended into civil war, and at least one neighbor reportedly called the police after hearing someone repeatedly yell, “CHECK THE TIMESTAMPS!”  Veteran competitor Tom wasted no time demanding a full-scale inquiry. “There’s absolutely no chance that dog was legitimately consumed,” Tom declared while dramatically pointing at a printed spreadsheet. “I want this entered into the Epween Files immediately. This has all the hallmarks of an advanced weenie laundering operation.” However, before investigators could retrieve the files, the entire Epween archive mysteriously vanished. Security footage from headquarters reportedly shows the filing cabinet simply… disappearing… at 1:33 a.m. Officials insist there was no sign of forced entry, though one witness claims to have heard “the faint sound of a Costco-sized package of hot dogs being zipped shut.”  The mystery deepened when eyewitness Nick came forward with a sworn statement that experts are already calling “deeply concerning.” “I woke up around 1:20 a.m. because I sensed a disturbance in the weeniverse,” Nick testified. “I looked over and saw Jake bolt upright in a cold sweat. He looked terrified. Then he grabbed something… cylindrical… held it near his mouth for several seconds… closed his eyes… and let out the most relieved sigh I’ve ever heard. It was like he’d just completed a mission.” Nick paused before adding, “I knew right then we were about to have a leaderboard problem.”  Digital forensic analysts have since uncovered additional anomalies. Jake’s Fitbit recorded a heart rate spike to 167 BPM at 1:27 a.m., followed by exactly 14 steps, a 47-second period of complete silence, and what investigators have labeled a “high-confidence chewing event.” Thermal imaging from a nearby Ring camera allegedly detected an unidentified warm object traveling across Jake’s kitchen at approximately 3.2 miles per hour, though scientists caution it could also have been “a determined raccoon.”  Meanwhile, Tom has doubled down, insisting this is merely the latest chapter in a sprawling conspiracy. “First the Epween Files disappear. Next thing you know, people will tell you hot dogs eat themselves. Wake up.” He has formally requested DNA testing of all remaining buns, subpoenaed the refrigerator light, and called for an emergency session of the International Court of Ween Arbitration.  Jake has refused to answer questions, issuing only a brief statement through his legal team: “No further comments will be made at this time. The ween speaks for itself.”  As of press time, the Late Night Ween remains on the scoreboard, though it now carries an unofficial asterisk, three question marks, and a warning label reading: “EXTREMELY CONTROVERSIAL.” Markets remain volatile, friendships are deteriorating, and experts agree the scandal may permanently alter the course of competitive hot dog history.  This story is developing. Assuming the evidence doesn’t mysteriously disappear again.. Nick was informed this had been submitted and asked whether the account was accurate. He said parts of it were. He was asked which parts. He said the parts that make him look okay. He was told those parts appear to be limited. He agreed, reluctantly.

tips_headlines.headline: 🌙🌭 “The 1:32 Incident” — Jake’s Midnight Ween Freezes Markets, Triggers Epween Archive Disappearance

tips_headlines.text: The Weenie Wars community was thrown into absolute chaos this morning after the anonymous release of the so-called “Epween Files,” a dossier alleging that current leader Tom, who sits atop the standings with 24 officially logged weenies, may have reached that total through methods the official rulebook describes as “deeply concerning and anatomically impossible.” Sources familiar with the investigation claim that as many as 85% of Tom’s recorded weenies never passed his lips at all, instead allegedly taking the “rear entrance express lane.” Officials confirmed that only weenies consumed orally are eligible to be logged, prompting an immediate review of Tom’s scorecard. Eyewitnesses reported seeing Tom “walking suspiciously stiff” after several meals, while one judge admitted, “At the time, we just thought he had bad posture. Looking back… there were signs.”  The allegations have sparked outrage across the competitive hot dog world. Rival competitor Nick demanded, “We’re here to celebrate athletic eating, not advanced storage solutions,” while Harrison declared, “I’ve never seen a man treat his digestive system like it has two front doors.” Devin, visibly confused during an impromptu press conference, reportedly shouted, “If the weenie disappears but nobody saw chewing, is it even a hot dog, or is it just a disappearing magic trick?” before wandering off in search of mustard. Meanwhile, investigators reportedly recovered enough circumstantial evidence to suggest dozens upon dozens of weenies may have embarked on what insiders are calling “The Brown Line Express.” Tom has vehemently denied all wrongdoing, insisting, “Every weenie I logged was consumed according to… my interpretation of the rules.” The Weenie Wars Commission has not announced a ruling, but insiders say officials are considering the largest score adjustment in league history if the Epween Files prove authentic. Until then, Tom’s 24-weenie lead remains under the darkest cloud ever cast over competitive tube-meat athletics.. Nick was informed this had been submitted and asked whether the account was accurate. He said parts of it were. He was asked which parts. He said the parts that make him look okay. He was told those parts appear to be limited. He agreed, reluctantly.

Until then, Tom’s 24-weenie lead remains under the darkest cloud ever cast over competitive tube-meat athletics.
tips_headlines.headline: 💩🌭 "The Brown Line Express" - Clubhouse Leader Tom Faces Explosive Keistering Allegations

tips_headlines.headline: 🏳️‍🌈🌭 Sources: Fenway Franks Deployed in Stroller at Pride Parade — Jake Named Person of Interest

tips_headlines.text: The world has officially entered Chorizogate after Owen traveled all the way to Uruguay and began insisting that chorizo is “basically just a hot dog.” Legal scholars have been working around the clock to interpret the Weenie Accords, while Nick and Tom have issued a unanimous ruling that this is a flagrant violation of international hot dog law—unless, and only unless, the chorizo is consumed from a singular, lonely chair facing the corner of a bedroom, in which case it’s merely a misdemeanor. Harrison remains the lone dissenter, passionately arguing before the court that “chorizo is absolutely a weenie,” despite overwhelming public outrage. Meanwhile, Devin contributed absolutely nothing to the legal discussion by declaring, “If you rotate the sausage 37 degrees clockwise, it becomes a breakfast item and therefore falls under bird law.” The tribunal adjourned without a verdict, but Owen continues wandering the streets of Uruguay eating suspiciously spicy “hot dogs” while the Hague prepares an arrest warrant.. Nick maintains that what happened is explainable. He has had multiple opportunities to explain it. His explanations have, to date, made the file larger rather than smaller. This is not typically how explanations work.

Harrison remains the lone dissenter, passionately arguing before the court that “chorizo is absolutely a weenie,” despite overwhelming public outrage. Meanwhile, Devin contributed absolutely nothing to the legal discussion by declaring, “If you rotate the sausage 37 degrees clockwise, it becomes a breakfast item and therefore falls under bird law.” The tribunal adjourned without a verdict, but Owen continues wandering the streets of Uruguay eating suspiciously spicy “hot dogs” while the Hague prepares an arrest warrant.
tips_headlines.headline: 🌶️⚖️ Owen Insists Chorizo 'Basically a Hot Dog'; Hague Prepares Arrest Warrant

This has been reported to the commission and logged in the Epween files.. The Commission forwarded this to the Epween Files archive. The archivist added it without comment, which colleagues noted was unusual — she normally has a comment. When pressed, she said only: this tracks. She was asked what it tracks. She said everything. She returned to the archive and closed the door.

tips_headlines.headline: 🕵️🤫 Anonymous Source Alleges Tom Operating Under Alias 'Just the Tip Tommy'

tips_headlines.text: Observed today - Nick walked out The Artificial Weenie factory and watched while 3 midgets loaded a pallet of unknown contents into his vehicle. Nick also laughed and pointed when one midget tripped on a blade of grass and face planted into a pile of dog feces. When an investigator for the Weenie Commission asked Nick if the pallet in his vehicle contained real weenies his response was “Per Se”. He then winked 3 times, did a pirouette, grabbed his groin and yelled “mmmm weenies for daddy!”. A group of children passing started crying and were sent to therapy.. Nick was informed this had been submitted and asked whether the account was accurate. He said parts of it were. He was asked which parts. He said the parts that make him look okay. He was told those parts appear to be limited. He agreed, reluctantly.

tips_headlines.headline: 🏭🌭 Nick Exits Artificial Weenie Factory with Mystery Pallet; Yells “Weenies for Daddy” After Pirouette

tips_headlines.text: There is a group forming calling themselves the lactose loyalists. They like cheese on their dogs. They were overheard discussing plans with John Cena for you can’t see me cheese so they could trick the other contestants into joining their cause without their knowledge. When the commission was informed there was one word used… diabolical. This was reviewed by the full Commission and flagged as actionable. The claim was cross-referenced with existing evidence and found to be corroborated on at least two points. The subject has since been described as cooperative, which is not the same as helpful.

When the commission was informed there was one word used… diabolical
tips_headlines.headline: 🧀👻 Lactose Loyalists Enlist John Cena for Invisible Cheese Plot; Commission Issues One-Word Verdict: Diabolical
Tom was seen at 2am on June 23rd walking to the Watcher in the corner of the room
tips_headlines.text: Tom was seen at 2am on June 23rd walking to the Watcher in the corner of the room. He was carrying a “large bottle of an unknown substance” which he claims was “just really thin mustard”. The bottle was found empty next to the watcher, however no evidence of mustard was recovered.. The item referenced above was identified by three Epween Files analysts independently and without coordination. All three reached the same conclusion. They shared their findings in a group message. The message contained no words. Just a single period. The period has been entered into the record.

He was carrying a “large bottle of an unknown substance” which he claims was “just really thin mustard”. The bottle was found empty next to the watcher, however no evidence of mustard was recovered.
